title: QMS BLUEPRINT
normalized_content:
PHASE 0.2 — QMS BLUEPRINT
Organization: Aetheris BioPharma Private Limited
Industry: Pharmaceutical Manufacturing (OSD, API, Sterile Injectables)
Foundation Reference: Phase 0.1 Organization Blueprint
Document Type: Structured plain text master QMS definition
Status: Foundational blueprint for future controlled documents
======================================================================
1. QMS MASTER PROFILE
======================================================================
Company Name:
Aetheris BioPharma Private Limited
QMS Scope:
The Aetheris Quality Management System applies to the design transfer, procurement,
manufacture, packaging, testing, release, storage, distribution, complaint handling,
investigation, CAPA, change control, validation, and continual improvement activities for
pharmaceutical APIs, oral solid dosage products, and sterile injectable finished products
manufactured at PASH and VISK sites.
QMS Sites in Scope:
- PASH = Aetheris Unit I – OSD & API Complex, Pashamylaram, Telangana
- VISK = Aetheris Unit II – Sterile Injectables, Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh
- CORP = Corporate functions supporting quality, regulatory, IT, HR, and governance from
Hyderabad
QMS Ownership:
- Executive Owner: Dr. Suresh Reddy, Chief Quality Officer
- Functional Owner: Meera Iyer, Head of Quality Assurance
- System Steward: S. Balaji, QA Manager – Systems
QMS Intent:
The QMS is designed to ensure product quality, patient safety, data integrity, regulatory
compliance, documented evidence generation, process control, investigation rigor, and
management oversight across the pharmaceutical product lifecycle.
QMS Core Principles:

- Patient safety first
- Right-first-time manufacturing
- Documented evidence for every critical activity
- Risk-based quality management
- ALCOA+ data integrity behavior
- Escalation of quality signals into formal investigations when required
- CAPA effectiveness verification
- Continuous improvement through trend review and management review
QMS Architecture:
Level 1 = Quality Manual
Level 2 = Corporate and Site Quality Policies
Level 3 = Standard Operating Procedures and Quality Procedures
Level 4 = Forms, templates, logs, records, master plans, protocols, reports
Level 5 = Raw evidence objects such as audit trails, chromatograms, batch records, training logs,
equipment logs, environmental monitoring records, and system event logs
======================================================================
2. QUALITY MANUAL
======================================================================
Quality Manual Identifier:
CORP-QA-POL-0001-R0
Document Title:
Aetheris Pharmaceutical Quality Manual
Document Owner:
Dr. Suresh Reddy, Chief Quality Officer
Document Custodian:
S. Balaji, QA Manager – Systems
Applies To:
All GxP functions and employees at PASH, VISK, and CORP supporting regulated manufacturing
operations.
Purpose:

To define the overall structure, intent, scope, governance, and interaction of the Aetheris
Pharmaceutical Quality System.
Quality Manual Structure:
Section QM-01: Company and QMS Scope
- Legal entity and site scope
- Product and process scope
- Exclusions and non-applicability rules
Section QM-02: Quality Policy and Management Commitment
- Commitment to GMP compliance
- Quality objectives
- Quality culture expectations
Section QM-03: Regulatory and Standards Framework
- India Schedule M
- WHO-GMP
- EU-GMP
- ICH Q7
- ICH Q9
- ICH Q10
- 21 CFR Part 11 aligned electronic record controls
- ISO 9001:2015 principles used for enterprise process discipline
- For VISK combination-product activities only, relevant elements of ISO 13485:2016 are applied
where required by customer or market expectations
Section QM-04: Organizational Responsibilities
- Authority of QA
- Independence of quality decision-making
- Management responsibilities
- Escalation pathways
Section QM-05: Document and Record Control
- Controlled-document lifecycle
- Revision control
- Retention, archival, retrieval, obsolescence handling

Section QM-06: Personnel, Training, and Competence
- Qualification by role
- GxP induction
- Periodic retraining
- Role-based training matrices
Section QM-07: Facilities, Utilities, and Equipment Control
- Qualification and maintenance
- Calibration
- Preventive maintenance
- breakdown management
- equipment status labeling
Section QM-08: Materials Management and Supplier Quality
- Supplier qualification
- receipt, quarantine, release, rejection
- status control and traceability
Section QM-09: Production and Process Control
- Batch documentation
- line clearance
- in-process controls
- reconciliation
- yield control
Section QM-10: Laboratory Control
- Sampling
- testing
- specifications
- OOS/OOT handling
- reference standards
- stability program
Section QM-11: Validation and Qualification
- CSV
- process validation
- cleaning validation
- analytical method validation

- transport validation
- sterile process simulation where applicable
Section QM-12: Deviations, Investigations, and CAPA
- Event detection and escalation
- investigation expectations
- root cause analysis methods
- CAPA initiation and effectiveness checks
Section QM-13: Change Control
- Temporary and permanent changes
- impact assessment
- approval workflow
- implementation and closure
Section QM-14: Complaints, Recalls, and Market Actions
- complaint intake
- triage
- investigation
- reporting
- recall governance
Section QM-15: Internal Audits and Management Review
- Self-inspection program
- external audit handling
- periodic management review
- quality metrics and trend analysis
Section QM-16: Data Integrity and Electronic Systems
- ALCOA+
- role-based access
- audit trails
- backup and restore
- incident handling
Section QM-17: Continual Improvement
- PQR/APR
- KPI review

- CAPA trends
- risk reduction initiatives
======================================================================
3. QUALITY POLICIES
======================================================================
The following Level 2 policies are foundational and mandatory across the enterprise. These
policies are not detailed SOPs; they state management intent, mandatory principles, and
ownership expectations.
3.1 Core Corporate Quality Policies
1. CORP-QA-POL-0001-R0
Title: Aetheris Pharmaceutical Quality Manual
Owner: Chief Quality Officer
Purpose: Defines the integrated QMS framework and governance.
2. CORP-QA-POL-0002-R0
Title: Corporate Quality Policy
Owner: Chief Executive Officer
Purpose: Declares commitment to quality, compliance, patient safety, and continuous
improvement.
3. CORP-QA-POL-0003-R0
Title: Data Integrity and ALCOA+ Policy
Owner: Chief Quality Officer
Purpose: Defines enterprise expectations for attributable, legible, contemporaneous, original,
accurate, complete, consistent, enduring, and available records.
4. CORP-QA-POL-0004-R0
Title: Good Documentation Practices Policy
Owner: Head of QA
Purpose: Defines rules for handwritten, hybrid, and electronic GxP documentation.
5. CORP-QA-POL-0005-R0
Title: Risk Management Policy
Owner: Chief Quality Officer

Purpose: Establishes risk-based quality decision-making aligned to ICH Q9
principles.[web:48][web:49]
6. CORP-QA-POL-0006-R0
Title: Training and Competency Policy
Owner: HR & Training Manager with QA approval
Purpose: Defines qualification, training assignment, retraining, and training effectiveness
expectations.
7. CORP-QA-POL-0007-R0
Title: Validation Master Policy
Owner: Chief Technology Officer with QA approval
Purpose: Defines lifecycle controls for qualification, validation, and revalidation.
8. CORP-QA-POL-0008-R0
Title: Supplier Quality Policy
Owner: Head of QA
Purpose: Defines supplier qualification, monitoring, periodic review, and disqualification
expectations.
9. CORP-QA-POL-0009-R0
Title: Quality Risk Escalation Policy
Owner: Chief Quality Officer
Purpose: Defines escalation thresholds for critical quality events, sterility risks, data integrity
concerns, and market actions.
10. CORP-QA-POL-0010-R0
Title: Audit and Inspection Readiness Policy
Owner: Head of QA
Purpose: Defines enterprise readiness for self-inspection, customer audits, and regulatory
inspections.
3.2 Site-Specific Quality Policies
11. PASH-QA-POL-0101-R0
Title: Pashamylaram Manufacturing Quality Governance Policy
Owner: Head of QA, PASH
Purpose: Defines site-level quality governance for OSD and API operations.

12. VISK-QA-POL-0101-R0
Title: Visakhapatnam Sterile Quality Governance Policy
Owner: Head of QA, VISK (functionally to Meera Iyer through site quality leadership structure)
Purpose: Defines site-level quality governance for sterile injectables, aseptic practices,
environmental monitoring, and contamination control.
13. VISK-QA-POL-0102-R0
Title: Sterility Assurance Policy
Owner: Site QA Sterile Operations Lead
Purpose: Defines contamination control strategy, aseptic behavior expectations, monitoring
principles, and sterility assurance responsibilities.
======================================================================
4. REGULATORY FRAMEWORK REGISTER
======================================================================
The Aetheris QMS is designed primarily for pharmaceutical manufacturing and is mapped to the
following standards and regulations.
Primary Regulations and Guidelines in Scope:
- India Drugs and Cosmetics Act and Rules
- India Schedule M for pharmaceutical manufacturing
- WHO Good Manufacturing Practices for pharmaceutical products
- EU GMP Guide for medicinal products
- ICH Q7 Good Manufacturing Practice Guide for Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients
- ICH Q9 Quality Risk Management
- ICH Q10 Pharmaceutical Quality System, which describes a model for an effective
pharmaceutical quality system across the lifecycle.[web:48][web:49][web:55]
- 21 CFR Part 11 principles for electronic records and signatures where Aetheris uses validated
electronic systems
- 21 CFR Part 210 and 211 principles for finished pharmaceutical CGMP expectations as
reference for export-market alignment.[web:44]
Contextual/Secondary Standards Used by Business Functions:
- ISO 9001:2015 for process management discipline and continual improvement principles
- ISO 13485:2016 applied selectively only where VISK sterile combination-product customer
requirements or device-related packaging interfaces make such controls relevant; FDA’s device

QMSR now incorporates ISO 13485:2016 by reference for medical device manufacturers, but
Aetheris is not positioning its core pharmaceutical QMS as a device QMS.[web:43]
Regulatory Library Control Record:
- CORP-RA-RPT-0001-R0 = Regulatory Intelligence and Standards Applicability Matrix
- CORP-QA-TMP-0001-R0 = QMS Regulation-to-Procedure Traceability Matrix
======================================================================
5. SOP LIBRARY ARCHITECTURE
======================================================================
SOP numbering uses the conventions established in Phase 0.1.
Format:
[Site]-[Area]-SOP-[AreaCode]-[Seq]-[Rev]
The SOP library below defines the initial controlled-document universe for the fictional
enterprise. It is organized by functional domain and remains internally consistent with Aetheris
sites, products, departments, and operations.

6. QUALITY PROCEDURES LIBRARY
======================================================================
These procedures are narrower operational procedures or governed quality workflows that
may exist as SOPs, work instructions, or templates depending on maturity. In the Aetheris
foundation, they are defined as quality procedures under controlled ownership.
QP-01 = Event Intake Procedure
Mapped document: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0107-R0
QP-02 = Investigation Planning Procedure
Mapped document: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0108-R0

Mapped document: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0109-R0

======================================================================
7. DOCUMENT TYPE REGISTER
======================================================================
The following controlled document classes are recognized in the Aetheris QMS.
POL = Policy
SOP = Standard Operating Procedure
FRM = Form

TMP = Template
LOG = Logbook or indexed operational log
RPT = Report
MST = Master list or register
VRP = Validation report/protocol family marker when needed
WI = Work Instruction
BMR = Batch Manufacturing Record
BPR = Batch Packaging Record
APR = Annual Product Review
PQR = Product Quality Review
======================================================================

======================================================================
9. TRACEABILITY RULES FOR FUTURE PHASES
======================================================================

The following constraints are now permanent for all future Aetheris documents:
- All future investigations must use the Phase 0.1 site codes and numbering rules.
- All future CAPAs must map back to this SOP and policy library.
- No future procedure may introduce a department, site, product, or employee that conflicts
with Phase 0.1 unless an explicit controlled organizational change is created.
- PASH documents may govern OSD and API operations only unless marked CORP.
- VISK documents govern sterile injectables and related support processes.
- Corporate documents govern enterprise systems, policies, metrics, and harmonized QMS
rules.
- Combination-product or device-adjacent controls at VISK must be clearly labeled as
supplemental to the pharmaceutical QMS rather than replacing it, consistent with the
company’s pharmaceutical core scope and the fact that FDA’s QMSR specifically applies to
finished device manufacturers.[web:43]
- The Aetheris QMS should primarily be described as a pharmaceutical quality system aligned to
ICH Q10 and GMP expectations rather than as a pure ISO 13485 device QMS.[web:48][web:49]
======================================================================
10. PHASE 0.2 COMPLETION STATE
======================================================================
Phase 0.2 now establishes:
- The enterprise quality manual structure
- The policy framework
- The regulatory applicability model
- The enterprise SOP library
- The quality procedures map
- The controlled document classes
- The master registers that future documents will populate
No investigations, deviations, OOS records, complaints, or CAPAs have been created in this
phase.
This phase defines the permanent QMS scaffold that future evidence artifacts must follow.
